oatmeal-and-banana-cake-argiro.name: Oatmeal And Banana Cake
instructions: We use a long form of 12×23 cm. We butter it and flower it well.
Melt the ripe bananas with a fork.
In a bowl, add the oil, sugar, vanilla, cinnamon, baking powder, baking soda and salt, and mix with wire.
Add the banana, honey and eggs, and beat with wire or hand mixer until homogenized.
Add the oats flour and mix with spatula.
In the end, add the chocolate and mix gently.
Transfer the mixture to the form and straighten the surface.
Bake in a well-heated ocean, at 170°C, in the suspensions, on the bottom grill, for about 1 hour and 10 minutes, until the blade of the knife comes out dry from the center.
Leave the cake on a rack for 15 minutes and then disfigure.
Pour with melted chocolate and decorate with banana slices.

old-charleston-style-shrimp-and-grits.name: Old Charleston-style Shrimp And Grits
instructions: Whisk grits into the boiling water, and then whisk in half-and-half. Reduce heat to medium-low and simmer, stirring occasionally, until grits are thickened and tender, 15 to 20 minutes. Set aside and keep warm.
Season shrimp with salt and cayenne pepper. Add lemon juice, toss to combine, and set aside to marinate.
Place sausage slices in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Cook, stirring occasionally, until browned, 5 to 8 minutes. Remove sausage from the skillet.

Add bacon to the same skillet. Cook over medium-high heat until evenly browned, about 5 minutes per side. Transfer bacon to paper towels to drain, then chop or crumble when cool enough to handle. Leave bacon drippings in the skillet.
Add bell peppers, onion, and garlic to the bacon drippings; cook and stir until onion is translucent, about 8 minutes.
Stir cooked sausage and marinated shrimp into the skillet with the cooked vegetables. Turn off the heat and set aside.

Melt butter in a small saucepan over medium heat. Whisk in flour, stirring to create a smooth roux. Turn heat to low and cook, stirring constantly, until mixture is golden brown in color, 8 to 10 minutes. Watch carefully, mixture burns easily.
Pour the roux over the sausage, shrimp, and vegetables.
Place the skillet over medium heat, add chicken broth, bacon, and Worcestershire sauce, and stir to combine. Cook until sauce thickens up and shrimp turn opaque and bright pink, about 8 minutes.
Just before serving, mix Cheddar cheese into grits; stir until cheese is melted and grits are creamy and light yellow.
Spoon grits onto plates and serve shrimp mixture over top.
Serve and enjoy!

old-fashioned-fudge.name: Old-fashioned Fudge

ingredients.item: . diamond crystal or  0.5 tsp. morton kosher salt
Chopped salted toasted nuts or flaky salt for topping

instructions: Mix 4 cups (800 g) sugar, 1 cup (84 g) Dutch-process cocoa powder, 1½ cups whole milk, 2 Tbsp. light corn syrup, and 1 tsp. Diamond Crystal or ½ tsp. Morton kosher salt in a large, heavy saucepan or Dutch oven fitted with a candy thermometer. Place over medium heat and cook, stirring often, until sugar is completely melted, 7–10 minutes. Increase heat to medium-high and allow the mixture to come to a boil. Cook until thermometer reads 234° F (soft-ball stage), 9–12 minutes. Remove from heat and stir in 6 Tbsp. unsalted butter and 2 tsp. vanilla extract. Let cool in the pot at room temperature without stirring until thermometer reads 110° F, 30–45 minutes. While the fudge is cooling, butter the bottom and sides of an 8" square pan and line with a strip of parchment paper that hangs over two sides to create a sling.

Beat fudge with a wooden spoon until it becomes very thick and loses its gloss (4–6 minutes of brisk beating; it’s okay to take quick, 30-second breaks as needed). You will know it’s ready when the fudge becomes very thick and starts to harden ever so slightly. Working very quickly, transfer fudge to prepared pan and press into an even layer using an offset spatula or clean hands. Top with chopped salted toasted nuts or flaky salt (if using); allow to cool uncovered at room temperature at least 4 hours, preferably overnight.
Cut fudge with a sharp knife, dipping the knife in hot water and wiping with a towel between cuts if necessary.
Do Ahead: Store fudge between layers of wax paper or parchment paper in an airtight container at room temperature up to 1 week.
Editor’s note: This fudge recipe first appeared on Epicurious in 2010 as ‘Pa’s Fudge.’ This version has been slightly adapted and updated for style by the Epicurious test kitchen. Head this way for more of our favorite Christmas candy recipes →

old-fashioned-lemonade.name: Old-fashioned Lemonade
instructions: Juice lemons; you should have 1 cup juice.
Combine juice, sugar, and water in a 1/2-gallon pitcher. Stir until sugar dissolves. Taste and add more water if desired.
Chill and serve over ice.

olive-oil-glasses-with-tomato-slice-and-.name: Olive Oil Glasses With Tomato Slice And Olive
olive-oil-glasses-with-tomato-slice-and-.original_name: Ποτηράκια ελαιόλαδου με φέτα ντομάτα και ελιά
instructions: Put the shots in order.
In each toothpick, take 1 piece of bread, 1 piece of tomato, 1 piece of feta cheese, 1 olive tree and add them to the shots.
Drizzle with olive oil, sprinkle oregano and salt and serve.

olive-orange-vinaigrette.name: Olive-orange Vinaigrette
instructions: Mix 1 thinly sliced shallot, 1/3 cup chopped pitted oil-cured black olives, 1/4 cup extra-virgin olive oil, 1/4 cup Sherry vinegar, 2 teaspoons finely grated orange zest, 1/4 cup fresh orange juice, and 1 teaspoon coarsely chopped aniseed in a small bowl. Season with kosher salt.

one-pan-chicken-fajita-rice-bowl.name: One-pan Chicken Fajita Rice Bowl
instructions: Preheat oven to 204°C (200°C).
Make the fajita seasoning: In a small bowl, mix together the salt, pepper cumin, garlic powder, and paprika.
Place the chicken, bell peppers, and onion on a baking sheet. Drizzle with the olive oil and sprinkle with the fajita seasoning.
Bake for 20-22 minutes, or until internal temperature of the chicken reaches 74°C (75°C).
Serve with wild rice and avocado, if desired. Leftover fajitas will keep in the refrigerator for up to 4 days.
Enjoy!

one-skillet-chicken-with-buttery-orzo.name: One-skillet Chicken With Buttery Orzo

ingredients.prep: bone-in chicken thighs (about 2 pounds total), patted dry
white and pale green parts only, chopped

instructions: Preheat oven to 400°. Rain salt and then some pepper all over chicken. Heat 2 Tbsp. butter in a medium cast-iron skillet over medium-high. Nestle chicken, skin side down, in skillet in a single layer with no gaps (if you can’t quite fit them all, wait until chicken shrinks slightly, then puzzle in the remaining pieces). Cook until meat is opaque around the edges and skin is deep golden brown, 6–8 minutes. Turn chicken skin side up and transfer skillet to oven; bake, uncovered, until chicken is cooked through, 10–15 minutes. Transfer chicken to a plate.

Set same skillet over medium; combine fennel bulb and leek in skillet and sprinkle in some salt and pepper. Cook, tossing occasionally, until leek is looking golden around the edges, about 5 minutes. Add orzo and cook until pasta is darkened (it will take on a brown hue) to a nice nutty brown in spots and toasty smelling, about 3 minutes. Pour in wine and cook, stirring, until liquid is evaporated, about 1 minute. Add broth ½ cup at a time, stirring constantly and letting broth absorb before adding more, until orzo is tender and broth is mostly absorbed but pan is not dry, 10–15 minutes.

Remove skillet from heat, Taste and add more salt and pepper to your liking; mix in lemon juice and remaining 1 Tbsp. butter, then chopped fennel fronds. Pile chicken on top and finish with lemon zest.

onion-pie-with-feta-cheese-argiro.name: Onion Pie With Feta Cheese
instructions: Clean the onions first and cut into thin slices.
Heat the olive oil in a deep pan or plastic pot, add the onions and, over medium heat, drizzle regularly without getting any color for about 20 minutes to soften.
Let them cool a little.
Add the feta cheese, beaten eggs, chives, salt and pepper.
In an oily shallow baking pan for pie No.36 spread 6 sheets of radially, the corners to cover the surface of the baking pan.
Sprinkle each leaf with a few drops of olive oil.
Apply half the filling evenly.
Lay two sheets of wrinkled leaves in between and spread the rest of the filling.
Turn the leaf edges onto the pie.
Lay the rest of the tarot leaves to cover the whole surface of the pie, sprinkle with some olive oil.
Sprinkle in pieces, sprinkle with some water with the fingers so that the baking sheet does not rise, pour with a little olive oil.
Bake the onion pie at 180

open-sandwich-argiro.name: Open Sandwich

ingredients.item: A few drops of balsamic vinegar
A few drops of balsamic vinegar

instructions: Cut the vegetables, eggplant and pumpkin into very thin slices.
Cut the pepper, remove the seeds and fibers and chop it into small frames.
Heat a pan over high heat and add the olive oil.
Add the vegetarians to the hot pan and sauce for 3-4 minutes, string over high heat.
Add salt and pepper at will.
Add the tomatoes cut in half, sauté for 2’
Add the balsamic and mix.
Remove the vegetables from the heat and add the parsley leaves.
Put the bread in a small baking pan.
Divide the grilled vegetables into the bread slices.
Cover with 2 slices of cheese.
Leave it for 5’ in the oven at 180 oC in the air to melt the cheese lightly.
Enjoy the warm and delicious.

ossobuco-with-polenta-teleculinaria.name: Ossobuco With Polenta
instructions: Arranje o ossobuco e tempere-o com o vinho branco, a folha de louro, o ramo de salsa, sal e pimenta.
Light a pot with the olive oil, let it warm, add the onion, garlic and tomato, all chopped up, stir and let it cook for 5 minutes, or until everything becomes soft.
Then add the ossobucus with the marinade, tape and let it cook, in mild heat, for about 45 minutes, or until the meat becomes soft. If necessary, add water slowly to get some sauce.
However, cook the polenta following the indications of the pack. Remove the ossobucus from the flame and serve with the polenta.

oven-roasted-asparagus.name: Oven-roasted Asparagus
instructions: Place asparagus into a mixing bowl, drizzle with olive oil, and toss to coat.
Sprinkle with Parmesan cheese and garlic; season to taste with salt and pepper if using. Arrange asparagus in a single layer in a baking dish or on a rimmed baking sheet.
Bake in the preheated oven until just tender, 12 to 15 minutes depending on thickness. Sprinkle with lemon juice just before serving, and serve immediately for the best texture.

own-mincemeat-pie-filling.name: Own Mincemeat Pie Filling
instructions: Cook and stir ground beef in a skillet over medium heat until well-browned, about 10 minutes, breaking it up into small crumbles as it cooks. Drain excess fat.

Combine beef, apples, brown sugar, brandy, raisins, suet, walnuts, dried mixed fruit, mixed fruit peel, lemon juice, salt, nutmeg, cinnamon, allspice, and cloves in a large cast-iron or other heavy skillet over medium heat. Stir until thoroughly combined and sugar has dissolved; bring to a boil. Reduce heat to a simmer; cook until flavors have blended and filling is thickened, about 1 hour. Stir occasionally.
Use mincemeat pie filling as desired.
